mission.title: Install llm-d Simulated Accelerators
mission.description: Deploy the llm-d accelerator simulator for CPU-only testing without real GPUs. The simulator mimics vLLM inference behavior, enabling validation of Kubernetes autoscaling, inference scheduling, and system behavior in wide or dense configurations on CPU-only machines.

steps.title: Install prerequisite client tools
steps.description:
Ensure you have:
- `helm` (v3+)
- `helmfile` (v0.169+)
- `kubectl` (v1.29+)
- `yq` (v4+)

See the [client setup guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup).

**Note:** No HuggingFace token is needed — the simulator stubs the vLLM server.

steps.title: Configure Gateway API
steps.description:
Ensure your cluster has Gateway API CRDs installed and a gateway provider deployed.

See the [gateway provider guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/gateway-provider).

steps.title: Deploy the simulator stack
steps.description:
```bash
cd guides/simulated-accelerators
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For specific gateway providers:**
```bash
helmfile apply -e kgateway -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Install HTTPRoute
steps.description:
**For Istio or kgateway:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For GKE:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.gke.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check Helm releases:
```bash
helm list -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: `gaie-sim`, `infra-sim`, `ms-sim` all in deployed status.

Verify pods:
```bash
kubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: 1 EPP pod, 1 gateway pod, 3 decode pods, 1 prefill pod.

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows 3 Helm releases (gaie-sim, infra-sim, ms-sim) and all simulator pods running without any GPU resources. The stack accepts inference requests and returns simulated responses.

uninstall.title: Destroy the helmfile deployment

upgrade.title: Update llm-d version
upgrade.description: Update the llm-d version in your helmfile configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Simulator pods not responding to inference requests
troubleshooting.description:
Check simulator pod logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/component=modelservice -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=100
```

Verify the HTTPRoute is applied and the gateway service is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Testing autoscaling with the simulator
troubleshooting.description: The simulator is ideal for testing Kubernetes autoscaling (HPA/KEDA) behavior. Combine with the [Workload Autoscaling guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/workload-autoscaling) using `simulated-accelerators` as the base deployment. Note: saturation-based scaling does not require GPU node patching.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster (no GPUs required), Gateway API CRDs, a gateway provider, and the monitoring stack.
